section.title: Viewport Breakpoints
section.subtitle: ob.g.mode_collection.viewport.breakpoint.<tier>
section.purpose: Lower boundary of each viewport range — the breakpoint primitives the range bounds reference.
section.guideline: Source of truth for the viewport tier boundaries. Use these primitives when authoring custom media queries.

section.title: Viewport Ranges
section.subtitle: ob.g.mode_collection.viewport.range.<tier>.{from,to}
section.purpose: Range bounds per tier. `from` is inclusive and equals the tier's breakpoint; `to` is inclusive and is one pixel below the next breakpoint. 2xl is open-ended and has no `to`.
section.guideline: Use the resolved value column for inclusive media-query bounds. Never overlap adjacent ranges.

section.title: Viewport – CSS-Selectors
section.subtitle: ob.g.mode_collection.viewport.css_selector.<tier>
section.purpose: Per-tier developer-facing CSS class. xl is the base — no class applied. Other tiers add .ob-viewport-<tier> to <body>.
section.guideline: Read in the dev's runtime viewport-detection layer; toggle on <body> to activate non-base tiers.

section.title: Viewport – Page Container Widths
section.subtitle: ob.s.dimension.viewport.{min_width, max_width}
section.purpose: Mode-scoped page-container min / max widths — the Figma-bindable layer consumed by frames.
section.guideline: Bind to the page container frame. Resolves per active viewport mode.

section.title: Viewport – Header Variant
section.subtitle: ob.c.header.variant
section.purpose: [FIGMA-ONLY] Temporary token used only for Figma documentation. Selects the _pseudo_header component variant per viewport mode — mobile below lg, desktop from lg up.
section.guideline: Delete this token (and the _pseudo_header) when the real header component ships.
